1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم هل أتى على الإنسان حين من الدهر لم يكن شيئا مذكورا
1.english: Has there [not] come upon man a period of time when he was not a thing [even] mentioned?
1.transliteration: Hal ataa 'alal insaani heenum minad dahri lam yakun shai'am mazkooraa

1.arabic: إنا خلقنا الإنسان من نطفة أمشاج نبتليه فجعلناه سميعا بصيرا
1.english: Indeed, We created man from a sperm-drop mixture that We may try him; and We made him hearing and seeing.
1.transliteration: Innaa khalaqnal insaana min nutfatin amshaajin nabta leehi faja'alnaahu samee'am baseeraa

1.arabic: إنا هديناه السبيل إما شاكرا وإما كفورا
1.english: Indeed, We guided him to the way, be he grateful or be he ungrateful.
1.transliteration: Innaa hadainaahus sabeela immaa shaakiranw wa immaa kafoora

1.arabic: إنا أعتدنا للكافرين سلاسل وأغلالا وسعيرا
1.english: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains and shackles and a blaze.
1.transliteration: Innaaa a'tadnaa lilkaa fireena salaasila wa aghlaalanw wa sa'eeraa

1.arabic: إن الأبرار يشربون من كأس كان مزاجها كافورا
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of Kafur,
1.transliteration: innal abraara yashra boona min kaasin kaana mizaa juhaa kaafooraa

1.arabic: عينا يشرب بها عباد الله يفجرونها تفجيرا
1.english: A spring of which the [righteous] servants of Allah will drink; they will make it gush forth in force [and abundance].
1.transliteration: 'Aynany yashrabu bihaa 'ibaadul laahi yafajjiroonahaa tafjeeraa

1.arabic: يوفون بالنذر ويخافون يوما كان شره مستطيرا
1.english: They [are those who] fulfill [their] vows and fear a Day whose evil will be widespread.
1.transliteration: Yoofoona binnazri wa yakhaafoona yawman kaana sharruhoo mustateeraa

1.arabic: ويطعمون الطعام على حبه مسكينا ويتيما وأسيرا
1.english: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive,
1.transliteration: Wa yut''imoonat ta'aama 'alaa hubbihee miskeenanw wa yatemanw wa aseeraa

1.arabic: إنما نطعمكم لوجه الله لا نريد منكم جزاء ولا شكورا
1.english: [Saying], "We feed you only for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude.
1.transliteration: Innaamaa nut'imukum li wajhil laahi laa nureedu minkum jazaaa'anw wa laa shukooraa

1.arabic: إنا نخاف من ربنا يوما عبوسا قمطريرا
1.english: Indeed, We fear from our Lord a Day austere and distressful."
1.transliteration: Innaa nakhaafu mir Rabbinna Yawman 'aboosan qamtareeraa

1.arabic: فوقاهم الله شر ذلك اليوم ولقاهم نضرة وسرورا
1.english: So Allah will protect them from the evil of that Day and give them radiance and happiness
1.transliteration: Fa waqaahumul laahu sharra zaalikal yawmi wa laqqaahum nadratanw wa surooraa

1.arabic: وجزاهم بما صبروا جنة وحريرا
1.english: And will reward them for what they patiently endured [with] a garden [in Paradise] and silk [garments].
1.transliteration: Wa jazaahum bimaa sabaroo janatanw wa hareeraa

1.arabic: متكئين فيها على الأرائك ۖ لا يرون فيها شمسا ولا زمهريرا
1.english: [They will be] reclining therein on adorned couches. They will not see therein any [burning] sun or [freezing] cold.
1.transliteration: muttaki'eena feeha 'alal araaa 'iki laa yarawna feehaa shamsanw wa laa zamhareeraa

1.arabic: ودانية عليهم ظلالها وذللت قطوفها تذليلا
1.english: And near above them are its shades, and its [fruit] to be picked will be lowered in compliance.
1.transliteration: Wa daaniyatan 'alaihim zilaaluhaa wa zullilat qutoofu haa tazleela

1.arabic: ويطاف عليهم بآنية من فضة وأكواب كانت قواريرا
1.english: And there will be circulated among them vessels of silver and cups having been [created] clear [as glass],
1.transliteration: Wa yutaafu 'alaihim bi aaniyatim min fiddatinw wa akwaabin kaanat qawaareeraa

1.arabic: قوارير من فضة قدروها تقديرا
1.english: Clear glasses [made] from silver of which they have determined the measure.
1.transliteration: Qawaareera min fiddatin qaddaroohaa taqdeeraa

1.arabic: ويسقون فيها كأسا كان مزاجها زنجبيلا
1.english: And they will be given to drink a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of ginger
1.transliteration: Wa yusawna feehaa kaasan kaana mizaajuhaa zanjabeelaa

1.arabic: عينا فيها تسمى سلسبيلا
1.english: [From] a fountain within Paradise named Salsabeel.
1.transliteration: 'Aynan feeha tusammaa salsabeelaa

1.arabic: ۞ ويطوف عليهم ولدان مخلدون إذا رأيتهم حسبتهم لؤلؤا منثورا
1.english: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal. When you see them, you would think them [as beautiful as] scattered pearls.
1.transliteration: Wa yatoofu 'alaihim wildaanum mukhalladoona izaa ra aytahum hasibtahum lu'lu 'am mansoora

1.arabic: وإذا رأيت ثم رأيت نعيما وملكا كبيرا
1.english: And when you look there [in Paradise], you will see pleasure and great dominion.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa ra ayta summa ra ayta na'eemanw wa mulkan kabeera

1.arabic: عاليهم ثياب سندس خضر وإستبرق ۖ وحلوا أساور من فضة وسقاهم ربهم شرابا طهورا
1.english: Upon the inhabitants will be green garments of fine silk and brocade. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink.
1.transliteration: 'Aaliyahum siyaabu sundusin khudrunw wa istabraq, wa hullooo asaawira min fiddatinw wa saqaahum Rabbuhum sharaaban tahooraa

1.arabic: إن هذا كان لكم جزاء وكان سعيكم مشكورا
1.english: [And it will be said], "Indeed, this is for you a reward, and your effort has been appreciated."
1.transliteration: Innaa haazaa kaana lakum jazz 'anw wa kaana sa'yukum mashkooraa

1.arabic: إنا نحن نزلنا عليك القرآن تنزيلا
1.english: Indeed, it is We who have sent down to you, [O Muhammad], the Qur'an progressively.
1.transliteration: Innaa nahnu nazzalnaa 'alaikal quraana tanzeelaa

1.arabic: فاصبر لحكم ربك ولا تطع منهم آثما أو كفورا
1.english: So be patient for the decision of your Lord and do not obey from among them a sinner or ungrateful [disbeliever].
1.transliteration: Fasbir lihukmi Rabbika wa laa tuti' minhum aasiman aw kafooraa

1.arabic: واذكر اسم ربك بكرة وأصيلا
1.english: And mention the name of your Lord [in prayer] morning and evening
1.transliteration: Wazkuris ma Rabbika bukratanw wa aseelaa

1.arabic: ومن الليل فاسجد له وسبحه ليلا طويلا
1.english: And during the night prostrate to Him and exalt Him a long [part of the] night.
1.transliteration: Wa minal laili fasjud lahoo wa sabbihhu lailan taweelaa

1.arabic: إن هؤلاء يحبون العاجلة ويذرون وراءهم يوما ثقيلا
1.english: Indeed, these [disbelievers] love the immediate and leave behind them a grave Day.
1.transliteration: Inna haaa'ulaa'i yuhibboona 'aajilata wa yazaroona waraaa'ahum yawman saqeelaa

1.arabic: نحن خلقناهم وشددنا أسرهم ۖ وإذا شئنا بدلنا أمثالهم تبديلا
1.english: We have created them and strengthened their forms, and when We will, We can change their likenesses with [complete] alteration.
1.transliteration: Nahnu khalaqnaahum wa shadadnaaa asrahum wa izaa shi'naa baddalnaaa amsaala hum tabdeelaa

1.arabic: إن هذه تذكرة ۖ فمن شاء اتخذ إلى ربه سبيلا
1.english: Indeed, this is a reminder, so he who wills may take to his Lord a way.
1.transliteration: Inna haazihee tazkiratun fa man shaaa'at takhaza ilaa rabbihee sabeela

1.arabic: وما تشاءون إلا أن يشاء الله ۚ إن الله كان عليما حكيما
1.english: And you do not will except that Allah wills. Indeed, Allah is ever Knowing and Wise.
1.transliteration: Wa maa tashaaa'oona illaa anyyashaaa'al laah; innal laahaa kaana'Aleeman Hakeema

1.arabic: يدخل من يشاء في رحمته ۚ والظالمين أعد لهم عذابا أليما
1.english: He admits whom He wills into His mercy; but the wrongdoers - He has prepared for them a painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Yudkhilu mai yashaaa'u fee rahmatih; wazzaalimeena a'adda lahum 'azaaban aleemaa
